# samirgrover.ai

> Samir Grover, MD, MEd, FRCPC, FASGE, AGAF — gastroenterologist, medical educator, and education researcher at the University of Toronto and Scarborough Health Network.

## About

Samir Grover is the Executive Vice-President of Academics at Scarborough Health Network and Associate Professor of Medicine (Gastroenterology) at the University of Toronto. He holds the Pialis Family Chair in Education Research at SHNRI. He is building SAMIH — the Scarborough Academy of Medicine and Integrated Health, UofT's 5th undergraduate medical academy, launching in 2027.

## Research Focus

AI in medical education, simulation-based education, procedural competency assessment, cognitive load, clinician-developer training. Creator of the Toronto Upper GI Cleaning Score (TUGCS). 120+ peer-reviewed publications, h-index ~30, ~3,300 citations.

## Signature Ideas

- **The Karpathy Endpoint**: The threshold at which AI capability meets or exceeds physician capability — and the redesign of medical education, professional identity, and health systems that must follow.
- **Neverskilling**: The risk that AI performs cognitive tasks FOR trainees instead of WITH them, causing skills to never be acquired in the first place.
- **The AI-Augmented Physician**: Physicians who use AI will replace those who don't. Training must prepare for collaboration, not competition.
- **Build, Don't Just Use**: Clinicians must become developers of AI tools, not just consumers.
